hypothesis: A generative model trained to jointly predict both crystal structure and its corresponding space group symmetry directly from XRD patterns—where the space group prediction is used at inference time to dynamically select and apply the appropriate symmetry projector during gradient-based refinement—will achieve higher structure recovery rates than either unconstrained optimization or optimization with externally provided symmetry constraints, because the model's learned uncertainty over space group assignments can be propagated as a soft constraint that regularizes the non-convex XRD loss landscape without requiring perfect symmetry classification as a prerequisite.
